claim: Doctors spend 10-20 hours per week on clinical documentation, including 'pajama time' (after-hours work in pajamas at home)
actionable: Use ambient AI documentation tools to reduce clinician administrative burden and improve work-life balance
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Over 90% of clinical alerts are ignored by healthcare providers due to alert fatigue
actionable: Design high-confidence alerts that intervene only when clinically critical, using context-aware decision-making to reduce false positives
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: The conversation between patient and clinician is the most important workflow in healthcare; nearly everything in healthcare (diagnosis, treatment, claims) derives from this conversation
actionable: Build AI products that enhance the patient-clinician conversation rather than replacing it, focusing on saving time before/during/after visits
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Context is king in healthcare AI—having access to patient history, care guidelines, and medical literature is essential for clinical decision support and preventing errors
actionable: Build context engines that integrate EHR data, patient history, relevant guidelines, and evidence to inform real-time clinical recommendations
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Abridge wants its product to feel like 'air conditioning'—always in the background making things better, only interrupting when clinically critical
actionable: Design AI systems that are proactive but non-intrusive, intervening only when the stakes are high and timing is critical
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Prior authorization delays care by weeks; real-time AI can approve procedures while the patient is still in the room, converting a 45-day process into minutes
actionable: Implement real-time AI agents that check insurance requirements and pre-requisites during clinical visits to enable instant prior authorization approval
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Abridge has access to 100+ million medical conversations, which serves as proprietary training data for specialized models better than general foundation models
actionable: Leverage domain-specific conversation data to train custom models for specialized tasks where general models underperform
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Real-time latency in healthcare AI requires a constellation of models—using cheap/fast models for triage and more intelligent models for complex decisions
actionable: Implement tiered model strategies where simple models filter/triage inputs and expensive models handle complex reasoning, balancing quality/latency/cost
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Clinicians require deep personalization at three levels: individual (style preferences), specialty (cardiology vs dermatology workflows differ greatly), and health system (local guidelines)
actionable: Build multi-level personalization: support style preferences, specialty-specific models/evals, and allow health systems to embed custom guidelines into AI products
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Specialty-specific evals are hard-earned and non-transferable—what defines a good dermatology note is very different from a primary care note, requiring separate calibration
actionable: Create domain/specialty-specific evaluation frameworks and hire domain experts (clinicians) to calibrate quality standards rather than using one-size-fits-all metrics
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Healthcare needs zero-error or near-zero-error evals and progressive rollout—the 80/20 rule does not apply in healthcare where edge cases can be fatal
actionable: Invest heavily in edge case evaluation and gradual rollouts with real customers rather than trying to achieve 'good enough' accuracy
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Embedding clinician-scientists (MDs who are also engineers) in product/research teams raises the bar for all decisions by ensuring clinical rigor and relevance
actionable: Hire clinician-scientists and embed them in core teams to ensure product decisions are both clinically valid and technically sound
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Regulatory tailwinds in healthcare (FDA guidance, interoperability mandates, government support for clinical decision support) are creating favorable conditions for AI deployment
actionable: Monitor regulatory updates (like January 2024 FDA guidance on clinical decision support) and design products to align with regulatory expectations proactively
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Some of the hardest AI problems are getting solved first in healthcare due to high stakes—models must achieve exceptional rigor on accuracy, latency, and safety
actionable: Apply healthcare AI techniques (rigorous evals, multi-model ensembles, latency optimization) to other high-stakes domains
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: AI writing without context feels like 'slop'—clinical notes from Abridge feel authentic because they include patient context and clinician guidance through memory systems
actionable: Use context and clinician feedback (stored as memory) to guide AI output quality rather than relying on generic language models
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: PDFs and written clarity are more important than ever in product development, even in the age of prototypes and AI—crisp written specifications help teams and context windows
actionable: Invest in detailed written product specifications and technical documentation rather than relying solely on prototypes, especially for complex multi-stakeholder products
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits

claim: Models are becoming increasingly agentic, especially for coding tasks—EHRs can be treated as file systems that agents query and manipulate in real-time
actionable: Design agentic workflows that treat domain systems (like EHRs) as queryable/manipulable structures rather than static data sources
source_title: Inside Abridge: The AI Listening to 100 Million Doctor Visits
